name: Michigan State University Extension Service -Wayne Detroit
description_short: MSU Extension provides free, unbiased financial and homeownership education through certified counselors, webinars, and in-person classes across Michigan.
description_long:
Michigan State University Extension Service's MI Money Health program is a university-based financial education initiative serving Michigan residents through trusted, evidence-based resources. Founded as part of MSU's land-grant mission, the program aims to ensure every Michigan resident has access to personal finance and homeownership education regardless of financial status. The organization employs certified housing and money management counselors who deliver education designed to be practical, easy to understand, and free from commercial bias. MI Money Health operates both statewide and through local offices, including Wayne Detroit area services.

MI Money Health offers a comprehensive range of free educational services including online and in-person classes, one-on-one counseling with certified experts, self-paced learning modules, foreclosure prevention assistance, homeownership education seminars, and money management guidance. Their programs address core topics like budgeting, retirement planning, home buying processes, and foreclosure options. Specialized programs include Rent Smart (financial preparation for renting) and estate planning discussions. The organization regularly hosts webinars, in-person workshops, and presentations tailored to emerging community issues. Resources are available in multiple formats: articles, videos, publications, online courses, and live events.

What distinguishes MI Money Health is its university affiliation and non-commercial structure, which eliminates conflicts of interest present at for-profit financial service providers. As a university extension service, the program is backed by research and academic rigor while remaining accessible to the general public. Their certified counselors undergo formal training through HUD or equivalent programs. The organization publicly reports program impact data, demonstrating measurable outcomes for participants. The breadth of services—from youth-focused programs (Smart Money Missions for ages 13-19) to estate planning conversations—reflects a lifecycle approach to financial wellness.

The primary limitation is that MI Money Health is education-focused rather than providing active financial products or services like debt settlement, credit repair, or loans. While they help people understand their options (including foreclosure alternatives), they cannot directly intervene in creditor negotiations or modify existing debts. Service availability may vary by geographic location within Michigan, and some specialized counseling may require scheduling in advance. For consumers needing immediate debt intervention or credit repair, this organization functions best as an educational foundation rather than a standalone solution.
pros: Certified housing and money management counselors with HUD or equivalent training
Completely free education and counseling—no fees, no hidden costs
University-backed program with published impact data and research validation
Multiple access formats: in-person classes, webinars, self-paced videos, articles, and one-on-one expert consultation
Specialized programs addressing specific life situations (Rent Smart, inheritance planning, youth financial literacy)
Foreclosure prevention assistance available to homeowners in crisis
Serves diverse audiences regardless of financial status or credit history
cons: Education-only model—cannot directly negotiate with creditors or repair credit files
Geographic service limitations within Michigan; specific Wayne Detroit availability unclear from website
Cannot provide emergency financial assistance, loans, or direct debt relief
Foreclosure counseling explains options but cannot prevent foreclosure directly
best_for: Michigan residents seeking free financial education before making major decisions (home buying, renting, budgeting)
Homeowners facing foreclosure who need to understand their legal options and alternatives
First-time home buyers wanting to understand the home purchase process and requirements
Young adults (13-19) and families wanting to build foundational money management skills
diagnosis: MI Money Health is best for Michigan residents seeking free, unbiased financial education from a trusted university source before making major financial decisions or facing housing crises. The key caveat is that this is an educational organization, not a debt intervention or credit repair service—it prepares consumers to make informed decisions but cannot directly modify debts, repair credit reports, or provide loans.
services: One-on-one financial counseling with certified advisors
One-on-one housing counseling with certified advisors
Mortgage foreclosure basics webinars and counseling
Money management classes and self-paced courses
Rent Smart program (rental financial preparation and tenant conduct)
Estate planning and family asset transition education
Youth financial literacy programs (Smart Money Missions Goosechase game)
Online articles, videos, and publications on personal finance topics
Custom community presentations on emerging financial issues
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
